title: themeda triandra var. brachyantha
intro: Themeda triandra var. brachyantha, also known as Karkade or Desert Rose, is a variety of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been associated with several health benefits, such as reducing fever, improving digestion, and alleviating respiratory issues. Themeda triandra var. brachyantha can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional African and Middle Eastern medicine.
